channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A tour of the Obama Presidential Center in Chicago
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center opens this week. But it isn't an official presidential library.
item.content:encoded: The Obama Presidential Center opens this week. But it isn't an official presidential library.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Kevin Warsh debuts as Fed chair, holding interest rates steady
item.description: Kevin Warsh held his first news conference since taking over as the Federal Reserve chair. With inflation at its highest level in three-plus years, Warsh and his colleagues held interest rates steady.
item.content:encoded: Kevin Warsh held his first news conference since taking over as the Federal Reserve chair. With inflation at its highest level in three-plus years, Warsh and his colleagues held interest rates steady.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Las Culturistas Culture Awards take the elitism out of awards shows
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Matt Rogers and Bowen Yang of the Las Culturistas podcast about their tongue-in-cheek "Culture Awards" broadcast.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Matt Rogers and Bowen Yang of the Las Culturistas podcast about their tongue-in-cheek "Culture Awards" broadcast.
item.dc:creator: Fio Geiran

item.title: Trump touts preliminary deal to end the war with Iran in press conference
item.description: President Trump gave a wide-ranging press conference Wednesday at the end of the G7 summit where he tackled questions about his tentative agreement with Iran.
item.content:encoded: President Trump gave a wide-ranging press conference Wednesday at the end of the G7 summit where he tackled questions about his tentative agreement with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Rising utility bills in Tucson have locals suspicious of data centers
item.description: Nationwide peoples electric bills are up an average of 30% in the last five years. A big rate increase in Tucson has locals blaming a new data center.
item.content:encoded: Nationwide peoples electric bills are up an average of 30% in the last five years. A big rate increase in Tucson has locals blaming a new data center.
item.dc:creator: Katya Mendoza

item.title: Uzbekistan's first World Cup appearance is a dream come true for players and fans
item.description: The World Cup debut of Uzbekistan signals the region's growing confidence on the global stage and, some argue, the arrival of a "golden generation" making its mark in sports, culture and science.
item.content:encoded: The World Cup debut of Uzbekistan signals the region's growing confidence on the global stage and, some argue, the arrival of a "golden generation" making its mark in sports, culture and science.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: A special election in England could eventually determine the next prime minister
item.description: The working class area where George Orwell set a 1937 book may chose the next U.K. prime minister. Manchester's center-left mayor is vying for a seat in Parliament – and a path to the PM's job.
item.content:encoded: The working class area where George Orwell set a 1937 book may chose the next U.K. prime minister. Manchester's center-left mayor is vying for a seat in Parliament – and a path to the PM's job.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Outreach workers weigh the risks of forcing homeless vets to get help
item.description: More than 30,000 US military veterans are homeless. The Trump Administration has promised new housing for vets, but one of Trump's executive orders is targeting homeless people.
item.content:encoded: More than 30,000 US military veterans are homeless. The Trump Administration has promised new housing for vets, but one of Trump's executive orders is targeting homeless people.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: What Wisconsin swing voters say about Trump, the Iran war and America at 250
item.description: Swing voters from Wisconsin share their thoughts on the Iran war, high prices and how they're feeling about America at its 250th year marker.
item.content:encoded: Swing voters from Wisconsin share their thoughts on the Iran war, high prices and how they're feeling about America at its 250th year marker.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: Former Iranian diplomat reacts to preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Seyed Hossein Mousavian, a former Iranian diplomat and nuclear negotiator who spent decades representing Iran, about President Trump's deal to end the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Seyed Hossein Mousavian, a former Iranian diplomat and nuclear negotiator who spent decades representing Iran, about President Trump's deal to end the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Linah Mohammad
